Document:
# Security Officer
“Don’t talk to me until I’ve had my morning cup of coffee and doughnut.”
- Regularly patrol the station or man [bold][color=#004d75]Security Checkpoints[/bold][/color] unless told otherwise, or there is an active threat.
- Must take on a [bold][color=#004d75]Cadet[/bold][/color] if ordered to by the [bold][color=#DB9D3B]Head of Security[/bold][/color], at which point they will be responsible for ensuring the [bold][color=#004d75]Cadet[/bold][/color] follows Space Law & Standard Operating Procedures and that the [bold][color=#004d75]Cadet[/bold][/color] does not wander off on their own.
A [bold][color=#004d75]Security Officer[/bold][/color] with an assigned [bold][color=#004d75]Cadet[/bold][/color], depending on the [bold][color=#004d75]Cadet[/bold][/color]’s behavior, can be held liable for security-related crimes a [bold][color=#004d75]Cadet[/bold][/color] may or may not commit.
It is advised, but not required, that [bold][color=#004d75]Security Officers[/bold][/color] patrol in pairs on [bold][color=#1E90FF]Code Blue[/bold][/color] or higher.
## Responsible For: - Training [bold][color=#004d75]Cadets[/bold][/color]. - Sentencing Prisoners for crimes under 15 minutes. - Keeping the Station safe.
Standard [bold][color=green]Code Green[/bold][/color] Equipment:
or
or
or
in
or
